name: Tyler City Employees
description_short: Tyler City Employees Credit Union is a Texas-based credit union established in 1953, serving over 3,100 members with $27.7M in assets through one branch in Tyler, TX.
description_long:
Tyler City Employees Credit Union was established in 1953 and is headquartered in Tyler, Texas. As a state-chartered credit union, it operates as a not-for-profit financial institution dedicated to serving its membership community. The credit union has grown to steward over $27.7 million in assets and maintains a single branch location at 3711 Frankston Highway in Tyler, with a staff of over 8 dedicated employees.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of consumer banking services including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), auto loans, personal loans, mortgage options, wire transfers, and IRA/KEOGH accounts. Members have access to modern digital banking conveniences such as online banking, a mobile app for iOS and Android, bill pay, remote deposit capture, and e-statements. The credit union also provides traditional services including drive-thru teller service and access to financial calculators and comparison tools.

Tyler City Employees Credit Union distinguishes itself through its deep community roots—serving the Tyler, Texas area for over 70 years—and its member-focused approach as a not-for-profit cooperative. The institution emphasizes competitive rates and low fees. The credit union maintains a Q&A community forum for member support and provides multiple channels for customer service, including phone support at (903) 592-8012 and online assistance.

As a small, single-branch credit union, Tyler City Employees serves a geographically limited membership base primarily in Tyler, Texas. While it offers core banking products and digital services, its limited branch network and asset size mean fewer resources and services compared to larger financial institutions. The credit union's focus on local service and member relationships appeals to those seeking personalized banking within the Tyler area. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Checking Accounts (Share Draft accounts with 1,500+ members)
Certificates of Deposit (CDs/Share Certificates with 100+ members)
Bill Pay and Online Loan Payments
pros: Established 70+ year track record (founded 1953) providing stability and community trust
Not-for-profit credit union structure emphasizes member benefits over shareholder profits
Offers competitive auto loans for new and used vehicles with online calculators
Provides mortgage loans with flexible options and online payment calculators
Modern digital banking including mobile app (iOS/Android), online bill pay, and remote deposit
IRA/KEOGH retirement accounts available for tax-advantaged savings
Drive-thru teller service for convenient in-person transactions
Member community Q&A forum for peer support and financial guidance
cons: Single branch location limits accessibility for those outside Tyler, Texas area
No ATM network mentioned (0 ATMs listed), requiring reliance on branch or partner networks
Limited asset base ($27.7M) compared to regional/national banks may restrict loan amounts or product depth
Currently closed lobby and drive-thru hours during profile documentation, suggesting operational constraints
Small membership base (3,100 members) limits economies of scale for competitive product pricing
best_for: Tyler, Texas residents seeking personalized, community-focused banking relationships
Credit union members wanting not-for-profit member-owned financial services
Borrowers seeking auto loans or mortgages from a local, established institution
Savers wanting CD products and retirement accounts (IRA/KEOGH) with local service
diagnosis: Tyler City Employees Credit Union is best for Tyler, Texas residents seeking community-based banking with personalized service, auto loans, and mortgage products from a stable, not-for-profit institution. The main caveat is its single-branch footprint and limited reach—those outside Tyler or needing extensive branch/ATM networks should consider larger regional alternatives.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
